title: Masala Instant Noodles Upgrade

subs.name: 50 g paneer, crumbled
subs.notes: makes it vegetarian — fry until golden before adding noodles

subs.name: a few drops of vinegar + a pinch of salt

steps.text: Boil 1.5 cups water and cook the noodles for 2 minutes — slightly underdone. Drain, toss with a few drops of oil to prevent sticking, and set aside. Save the tastemaker sachet.

Heat oil in a kadai or frying pan on high flame. Add garlic and green chilli and fry 15 seconds until fragrant. Add onion and capsicum and stir-fry on high for 2 minutes until the edges just begin to char.
Push the vegetables to one side. Crack the egg into the pan and scramble it quickly until just set, then break it up and mix through the vegetables.

Add the drained noodles, half the tastemaker sachet, red chilli powder, soy sauce, ketchup and black pepper. Toss on high flame for 1–2 minutes until everything is coated and the noodles pick up a little colour.
Taste and adjust salt. Scatter spring onion on top and serve immediately while hot — the noodles get sticky if they sit.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: egg → 50 g paneer, crumbled (makes it vegetarian — fry until golden before adding noodles); soy-sauce → a few drops of vinegar + a pinch of salt (if soy sauce is unavailable); capsicum → cabbage or carrot, shredded (any quick-cooking vegetable works)
